require-no-change.description: Blocks any changed/added lines — requires the most concise solution (zero changes).
require-no-change.shell: CHANGED=$({ git diff HEAD --numstat; git ls-files --others --exclude-standard|while read f; do printf "%s\t0\t%s\n" "$(wc -l<"$f" 2>/dev/null)" "$f"; done; }|awk '$1~/^[0-9]+$/{a+=$1;d+=$2}END{print a+d+0}'); [ "$CHANGED" -eq 0 ] || exit 1

require-more-deletions.description: Requires more deletions than additions (e.g. for a refactoring).
require-more-deletions.shell: A_D=$({ git diff HEAD --numstat; git ls-files --others --exclude-standard|while read f; do printf "%s\t0\t%s\n" "$(wc -l<"$f" 2>/dev/null)" "$f"; done; }|awk '$1~/^[0-9]+$/{a+=$1} $2~/^[0-9]+$/{d+=$2} END{print a+0, d+0}'); set -- $A_D; [ "$1" -lt "$2" ] || exit 1

diff-max-10-lines.description: Blocks solutions changing/adding more than 10 lines.
diff-max-10-lines.shell: CHANGED=$({ git diff HEAD --numstat; git ls-files --others --exclude-standard|while read f; do printf "%s\t0\t%s\n" "$(wc -l<"$f" 2>/dev/null)" "$f"; done; }|awk '$1~/^[0-9]+$/{a+=$1;d+=$2}END{print a+d+0}'); [ "$CHANGED" -le 10 ] || exit 1

diff-max-50-lines.description: Blocks solutions changing/adding more than 50 lines.
